tagline: Spray polyurethane foam — seamless insulation and waterproofing in one system.
metaDescription: SPF spray foam roofing in Los Angeles County — seamless, energy-efficient, self-flashing. Free inspections. CA Lic. #1109337.
intro: SPF roofing is a seamless, monolithic system sprayed directly onto your roof deck. It delivers superior insulation (R-6+ per inch), self-flashes around every penetration, and — with proper coating maintenance — can last 30+ years. Palm Crest Roofing installs SPF on commercial, residential, and mixed-use buildings across LA County.
benefits: Seamless, self-flashing — no seams to fail at penetrations
R-6+ per inch — the highest R-value per inch of any roofing insulation
Lightweight — typically under 1 lb per square foot
Installs over most existing roof types without tear-off
30+ year service life with periodic coating recoats
Reflective elastomeric top coat for cool-roof performance

process.title: Inspection
process.desc: Assess existing roof for moisture, structural issues, and substrate suitability.

process.title: Prep & Repair
process.desc: Clean the surface, repair blisters and punctures, ensure a sound substrate.

process.title: Foam Application
process.desc: Two-component polyurethane is spray-applied, expanding and curing within seconds into a seamless insulating layer.

process.title: Elastomeric Coating
process.desc: Reflective top coat is applied over the foam for UV protection, waterproofing, and reflectivity.

process.title: Granule Broadcast
process.desc: Optional silica or ceramic granule broadcast for walkability and additional UV resistance.

faqs.q: How long does an SPF roof last?
faqs.a: The foam itself can last 30–50 years. The elastomeric coating needs to be recoated roughly every 10–15 years to maintain UV and waterproof performance.

faqs.q: Can SPF be installed over my existing roof?
faqs.a: Usually yes — SPF works over most existing roof substrates including built-up, modified bitumen, metal, and concrete, as long as the substrate is sound and dry.
Yes — significantly. SPF adds R-value and the reflective top coat reduces cooling loads. Many customers see 20–30% cooling cost reductions.
